danger_signals.signal: Enemy troop presence in adjacent districts
danger_signals.visual_form: Military unit icons/markers on map grid showing enemy army positions
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat of invasion or attack; districts at risk of being annexed

danger_signals.visual_form: Quarterly budget interface displaying insufficient funds for military spending
danger_signals.meaning: Reduced capacity to defend territories or wage offensives; vulnerability to enemy advance

danger_signals.visual_form: Political approval rating system feedback in Continental Congress/Parliament interface
danger_signals.meaning: Risk of ouster as Commander-in-Chief; loss of political legitimacy and command authority

danger_signals.visual_form: Patriot/Loyalist militia regiments moving or attacking on map without player authorization
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of district control; militia operating independently poses unpredictable military threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Hex-grid battlefield display showing open terrain where cavalry can maneuver freely
danger_signals.meaning: Tactical disadvantage; susceptibility to shock cavalry charges and devastating attacks

safety_signals.visual_form: Forts and towns under player control displayed on district map
safety_signals.meaning: Defensible strongpoints; protection against enemy invasion and resource security

safety_signals.visual_form: Quarterly budget interface showing adequate military spending approval
safety_signals.meaning: Capacity to train troops, purchase materials, maintain forces; strategic flexibility

safety_signals.visual_form: Political approval ratings in Continental Congress/Parliament interface showing positive values
safety_signals.meaning: Secure command authority; continued funding and public backing; low ouster risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly military unit markers in neighboring districts ready to reinforce battles
safety_signals.meaning: Available reinforcements; ability to defend threatened areas with additional forces

safety_signals.visual_form: Patriot or Loyalist militia under direct commander authority on map
safety_signals.meaning: Predictable asset; resource-generating units that expand territory autonomously

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Continental Congress representation; historical American Revolutionary insignia
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Patriot militia regiments (irregular forces fighting for American independence)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Districts controlled by American commanders; Continental Congress meeting interface

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: British Parliament representation; historical British military insignia
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Loyalist militia regiments (irregular forces supporting British Crown); German mercenary regiments
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Districts controlled by British commanders; Parliament meeting interface

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Self-organizing irregular units that fight and collect resources independently
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Militia units displayed on districts; recruitment indicators when regular commanders enter

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: British-aligned faction markers; mercenary unit designations
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Foreign and domestic irregular units supporting British cause
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Militia units displayed on British-controlled districts; mercenary payment indicators

world_state_to_visual.world_state: American strategic phase - Defensive holding pattern
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: American player controls fewer districts; British forces positioned aggressively on map; defensive fort indicators prominent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: American commanders focus on holding positions and slowly gathering strength; emphasis on attrition defense rather than offensive maneuvers

world_state_to_visual.world_state: British strategic phase - Fortification and rapid offense
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: British player controls more established territories with fortified positions; aggressive unit positioning visible on map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: British commanders prioritize quick strikes against American forces; fortify existing positions; naval barrages available in coastal districts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Budget allocation interface shows reduced military spending; approval rating UI displays declining numbers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player faces threat of being voted out as Commander-in-Chief; reduced capacity to train troops or purchase materials

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple friendly unit icons clustered in key districts; enemy forces spread thin on map display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ability to conduct offensive operations; opportunity to eliminate enemy troops in battles; expansion of controlled territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hex-grid battlefield terrain displays seasonal variations in appearance; impassable terrain indicators shift
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unit movement capabilities change; cavalry effectiveness reduced in difficult terrain; guerrilla units maintain superior mobility

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Naval force positioning in coastal districts; naval barrage range indicators on battlefield
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ability to transport troops to coastal battles; naval barrages supplement land-based combat; control of maritime access affects district vulnerability

environmental_storytelling.example: Player-controlled vs. enemy-controlled districts display clear visual distinction on strategic map; shows territorial progression of war
Patriot/Loyalist militia units visibly operate, attack, and annex adjacent districts without player input; illustrates grassroots nature of Revolutionary conflict

Six historically accurate Commanders-in-Chief (Washington, Ward, Lee for Americans; Gage, Clinton, Howe for British) represent actual war leadership; visual representation grounds simulation in real history
Quarterly Continental Congress/Parliament meetings display political entities approving/restricting military spending; reflects civilian-military relations and political constraints on war effort
Hex-grid terrain variety (open fields, forests, coastal areas) with seasonal changes tells story of American geography and environmental challenges to different unit types
Defenders must protect forts and towns while attackers attempt capture; reinforces strategic importance of fixed positions in Revolutionary War context

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Focus on defensive holding; slowly gather strength; avoid direct confrontation with superior British forces; prioritize popular support and budget approval
Conduct rapid offensive operations; fortify captured territories; apply military pressure to eliminate American forces quickly
Fight on their own; collect resources from districts; attack and annex adjacent districts; automatically place under regular commander authority if equal or higher rank enters district
Fight for British cause; collect resources; operate independently unless commanded; provide additional British military assets without direct player management
Approve or restrict military budget; judge Commander-in-Chief performance; vote for ouster in worst-case scenarios; directly impact available resources and command authority
Place armies on battlefield based on district entry point; maneuver troops strategically; call on allied troops from adjacent districts; utilize terrain and unit-type advantages
Move through terrain impassable to regular infantry; execute surprise attacks; provide tactical flexibility in difficult geographic areas
Require open terrain for movement; execute devastating shock charges against foot troops; vulnerable in difficult terrain
Conduct independent strategy unless directed; transport troops to coastal battles; deliver naval barrages against coastal defenders

notes: Liberty or Death communicates world-state primarily through turn-based strategy interface elements rather than dynamic visual manifestation. District control maps show territorial balance visually. Political legitimacy operates as hidden world-state mechanic—player cannot always perceive popular support levels until Congressional/Parliamentary meetings occur. Military asymmetry between American defensive holding and British offensive strategy creates distinct gameplay manifestations based on faction choice. The game's complexity and 250+ hour playtime suggest extensive world-state tracking through numerical systems not visually represented, indicating tension between underlying simulation detail and visual communication. Seasonal terrain changes and unit-type efficacy variations create tactical world-state variations.
